comments.author: backlog
comments.text: Verified in current checkout as an architectural consistency issue. Tool metadata is generally provided via TypeBox, but runtime param decoding is split across multiple systems: agent/tool.ts, memory/index.ts, skill-manage/index.ts, and exa/index.ts define TypeBox parameter metadata plus separate Effect Schema decoders via Schema.decodeUnknownSync(...); thread/index.ts instead uses TypeBox metadata and runtime decoding via Value.Parse(...) from @sinclair/typebox/value. So there is a real split-brain boundary today: two different runtime decoder systems are in active use for tool params, while TypeBox remains the SDK-facing metadata source. This is not an immediate bug if schemas stay aligned, but it does create schema drift risk and inconsistent error behavior.

comments.author: backlog
comments.text: Unified tool runtime parameter decoding on TypeBox `Value.Parse(...)` so runtime validation now comes from the same schemas used for tool metadata. Updated: `extensions/tau/src/agent/tool.ts`, `extensions/tau/src/memory/index.ts`, `extensions/tau/src/skill-manage/index.ts`, and `extensions/tau/src/exa/index.ts` to remove Effect Schema param mirrors and decode via TypeBox. Also replaced `Type.Unsafe` string-enum wrappers in memory/skill_manage and agent action with explicit literal unions to keep TypeBox parsing compatible. Tightened Exa TypeBox metadata for `type` and `livecrawl` to literal unions to preserve prior runtime constraints. Added regression tests: `extensions/tau/test/exa-tool-decoding.test.ts` (TypeBox constraints enforced pre-execution) and `extensions/tau/test/skill-manage-tool.test.ts` (decode boundary + validation flow). Validation: `npm run typecheck` and full `npm run gate` pass.
